skill.name: sharp-edges
skill.description: Identify error-prone APIs and dangerous configurations
skill.summary: Identify error-prone APIs and dangerous configurations
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported hardcoded URLs and weak cryptographic algorithm patterns, but review found no executable code, cryptographic API calls, or runtime network behavior. The URLs are repository references, and the crypto-related hits are descriptive security-review language. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Repository URL References
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL findings point to GitHub source and resource links. These are documentation references, not runtime network requests or data exfiltration paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines contain only static GitHub links in markdown metadata and resources. No evidence found of network execution or secret transmission.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Security Review Terminology
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm findings are keyword matches in descriptive text about identifying risky APIs and configurations. The file does not implement or recommend MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or other weak algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are plain-language descriptions of risk assessment goals. No evidence found of cryptographic code, configuration, or guidance that enables weak crypto.

content.value_statement: Risky APIs and unsafe settings can create defects that are hard to find during normal review. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to identify sharp edges and recommend safer choices.
actual_capabilities: Reviews code for APIs with risky parameters, unclear failures, or lifecycle requirements.
Checks configuration choices for insecure defaults, bypassed controls, and reliability hazards.
Flags concurrency, timing, resource management, and error handling concerns.
Explains the potential impact of each identified issue.
Recommends safer alternatives or documentation improvements when available.
limitations: It provides review guidance, not automated static analysis results.
It depends on the code and configuration context supplied by the user.
It does not execute tests, inspect live systems, or verify runtime behavior.
It may miss framework-specific pitfalls without project documentation.

use_cases.title: Review a Risky Integration
use_cases.description: Find dangerous API usage before merging a new service, library, or payment integration.

use_cases.title: Audit Production Settings
use_cases.description: Check configuration files for unsafe defaults, disabled controls, and reliability problems.

use_cases.title: Improve Developer Guidance
use_cases.description: Document common pitfalls and safer usage patterns for internal APIs.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Sharp Edge Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a small code change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this code for error-prone APIs and dangerous configuration choices. List each concern, explain the risk, and suggest a safer option.

prompt_templates.title: Configuration Safety Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit these configuration files for insecure defaults, bypassed controls, reliability hazards, and confusing options. Prioritize findings by likely impact.

prompt_templates.title: API Design Risk Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review before publishing an internal API.
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this API design for sharp edges. Focus on parameter safety, failure modes, resource ownership, concurrency behavior, and error handling.

prompt_templates.title: Security Review Memo
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review for a release decision.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a release review memo for these changes. Identify sharp edges, affected components, likely failure scenarios, required mitigations, and residual risk.

output_examples.input: Review a file upload endpoint that accepts user files and writes them to shared storage.
output_examples.output: The review flags unclear size limits, weak path handling, missing content validation, and unsafe storage defaults. It recommends explicit limits, normalized paths, type checks, and restricted storage permissions.

output_examples.input: Audit a service configuration before deployment.
output_examples.output: The audit highlights debug mode, permissive cross-origin settings, missing timeout values, and retries without backoff. It explains impact and proposes safer production defaults.

output_examples.input: Assess an internal client library before other teams adopt it.
output_examples.output: The assessment identifies ambiguous ownership rules, hidden retry behavior, and unclear error categories. It recommends clearer method names, documented failure modes, and safer defaults.

best_practices: Provide the relevant code, configuration, and expected runtime environment.
Ask for impact, likelihood, and safer alternatives for each issue.
Convert confirmed findings into tests, documentation, or configuration changes.
anti_patterns: Do not treat every warning as exploitable without context.
Do not review isolated snippets when surrounding configuration changes the risk.
Do not ignore reliability sharp edges because they are not direct vulnerabilities.

faq.question: What does this skill review?
faq.answer: It reviews APIs, configuration files, and design choices for patterns that can cause bugs, vulnerabilities, or failures.

faq.question: Is this a replacement for static analysis tools?
faq.answer: No. It complements automated tools by adding context, intent, and safer design recommendations.

faq.question: Can it find security vulnerabilities?
faq.answer: It can identify risky patterns that may become vulnerabilities, especially unsafe defaults and API misuse.

faq.question: What input should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the code, configuration, related documentation, and the environment where the system will run.

faq.question: Does it execute code or access the network?
faq.answer: No. The skill is instructional and does not include scripts, executable code, or network calls.

faq.question: Who benefits most from this skill?
faq.answer: Security reviewers, DevOps engineers, platform teams, and developers preparing changes for production use.
